message:
fix(codegen): Use class type directly for hoisted class constructor calls

When hoisting variables initialized with class constructor calls like
`a = ClassName(args)`, use the class type directly instead of
`@TypeOf(ClassName.init(args))`.

This prevents compile-time evaluation of constructor calls that might
throw errors (like `Rat(1, 0)` in try blocks expecting ZeroDivisionError).

Also added isBuiltinException helper to distinguish user classes from
Python builtins like TypeError, ValueError, etc.
